originContent: A lightweight web UI for monitoring and controlling [Gluetun](https://github.com/qdm12/gluetun) — the VPN client container for Docker.
translatedContent: A lightweight web UI for monitoring and controlling [Gluetun](https://github.com/qdm12/gluetun) — the VPN client container for Docker.

originContent: - ✨ **Multi-VPN Support** — Monitor & control up to 20 Gluetun instances simultaneously
translatedContent: - ✨ **Multi-VPN Support** — Monitor & control up to 20 Gluetun instances simultaneously

originContent: - Live VPN status banner (connected / paused / disconnected)
translatedContent: - Live VPN status banner (connected / paused / disconnected)

originContent: - Public exit IP, country, region, city, and organisation
translatedContent: - Public exit IP, country, region, city, and organisation

originContent: - VPN provider, protocol (WireGuard / OpenVPN), server details
translatedContent: - VPN provider, protocol (WireGuard / OpenVPN), server details

originContent: - Last 30 poll ticks colour-coded in history bar
translatedContent: - Last 30 poll ticks colour-coded in history bar

originContent: - Gluetun running with its HTTP control server enabled (default port `8000`)
translatedContent: - Gluetun running with its HTTP control server enabled (default port `8000`)

originContent: - Gluetun and gluetun-webui on the same Docker network
translatedContent: - Gluetun and gluetun-webui on the same Docker network

originContent: > Supports `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64` (works on Mac Intel/Apple Silicon, Linux, and Windows).
translatedContent: > Supports `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64` (works on Mac Intel/Apple Silicon, Linux, and Windows).

originContent: Add `gluetun-webui` to your existing compose file alongside Gluetun:
translatedContent: Add `gluetun-webui` to your existing compose file alongside Gluetun:

originContent: # Uncomment if Gluetun auth is enabled:
translatedContent: # Uncomment if Gluetun auth is enabled:

originContent: Monitor 2+ Gluetun instances with separate dashboards:
translatedContent: Monitor 2+ Gluetun instances with separate dashboards:

originContent: Both Gluetun and gluetun-webui must be on the same Docker network so `http://gluetun:8000` resolves correctly.
translatedContent: Both Gluetun and gluetun-webui must be on the same Docker network so `http://gluetun:8000` resolves correctly.

originContent: **Same compose file** — just add both services to the same network (most common):
translatedContent: **Same compose file** — just add both services to the same network (most common):

originContent: **Separate compose files** — reference Gluetun's existing network as external. Find your network name with `docker network ls`:
translatedContent: **Separate compose files** — reference Gluetun's existing network as external. Find your network name with `docker network ls`:

originContent: gluetun-webui supports monitoring and controlling **multiple Gluetun instances simultaneously**. Each instance displays as a separate dashboard in a responsive grid.
translatedContent: gluetun-webui supports monitoring and controlling **multiple Gluetun instances simultaneously**. Each instance displays as a separate dashboard in a responsive grid.

originContent: **Responsive**: 1 full-width dashboard → 2 half-width → 3 third-width → 4 quarter-width → scrollable at 5+
translatedContent: **Responsive**: 1 full-width dashboard → 2 half-width → 3 third-width → 4 quarter-width → scrollable at 5+

originContent: If no numbered variables are configured, falls back to **legacy single-instance mode**:
translatedContent: If no numbered variables are configured, falls back to **legacy single-instance mode**:

originContent: Each instance can have different authentication:
translatedContent: Each instance can have different authentication:

originContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_URL` | – | Gluetun HTTP control server URL for instance N |
translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_URL` | – | Gluetun HTTP control server URL for instance N |

originContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Bearer token for instance N (if auth enabled) |
translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Bearer token for instance N (if auth enabled) |

originContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_USER` | _(empty)_ | Username for HTTP Basic auth (instance N) |
translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_USER` | _(empty)_ | Username for HTTP Basic auth (instance N) |

originContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_PASSWORD` | _(empty)_ | Password for HTTP Basic auth (instance N) |
translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_{N}_PASSWORD` | _(empty)_ | Password for HTTP Basic auth (instance N) |

originContent: | `GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL` | `http://gluetun:8000` | **Legacy** – single instance only (fallback if no `GLUETUN_1_*` vars) |
translatedContent: | `GLUETUN_CONTROL_URL` | `http://gluetun:8000` | **Legacy** – single instance only (fallback if no `GLUETUN_1_*` vars) |

originContent: | `PORT` | `3000` | Port the web UI listens on |
translatedContent: | `PORT` | `3000` | Port the web UI listens on |

originContent: | `TRUST_PROXY` | `false` | Set to `true` if running behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Traefik, etc.) |
translatedContent: | `TRUST_PROXY` | `false` | Set to `true` if running behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Traefik, etc.) |

originContent: - Port is bound to `127.0.0.1` — not exposed to the network
translatedContent: - Port is bound to `127.0.0.1` — not exposed to the network

originContent: - Container runs as non-root with read-only filesystem and dropped capabilities
translatedContent: - Container runs as non-root with read-only filesystem and dropped capabilities

originContent: - Rate limiting applied to all API routes
translatedContent: - Rate limiting applied to all API routes

originContent: - Upstream error details are logged server-side only — generic messages returned to the browser
translatedContent: - Upstream error details are logged server-side only — generic messages returned to the browser

originContent: If you run gluetun-webui behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Traefik, Caddy, etc.), set `TRUST_PROXY=true` in your environment variables:
translatedContent: If you run gluetun-webui behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Traefik, Caddy, etc.), set `TRUST_PROXY=true` in your environment variables:

originContent: This allows the app to correctly parse `X-Forwarded-For` and related headers for accurate rate limiting and IP detection. **Note:** Only enable this if you're actually behind a reverse proxy, as it trusts proxy headers from your reverse proxy.
translatedContent: This allows the app to correctly parse `X-Forwarded-For` and related headers for accurate rate limiting and IP detection. **Note:** Only enable this if you're actually behind a reverse proxy, as it trusts proxy headers from your reverse proxy.

originContent: The VPN start/stop controls have no built-in authentication. If you expose the UI beyond localhost, place it behind a reverse proxy with HTTP Basic auth.
translatedContent: The VPN start/stop controls have no built-in authentication. If you expose the UI beyond localhost, place it behind a reverse proxy with HTTP Basic auth.

originContent: Generate a password file with: `htpasswd -c /etc/nginx/.htpasswd user`
translatedContent: Generate a password file with: `htpasswd -c /etc/nginx/.htpasswd user`

originContent: Generate a hash with: `htpasswd -nb user password`
translatedContent: Generate a hash with: `htpasswd -nb user password`

originContent: - **[Gluetun](https://github.com/qdm12/gluetun)** — The VPN client container this webui was built for
translatedContent: - **[Gluetun](https://github.com/qdm12/gluetun)** — The VPN client container this webui was built for

originContent: - **[gluetun-monitor](https://github.com/csmarshall/gluetun-monitor)** — Great monitoring tool to pair with this webui
translatedContent: - **[gluetun-monitor](https://github.com/csmarshall/gluetun-monitor)** — Great monitoring tool to pair with this webui

originContent: - **AI-Assisted Development** — This project was built with AI assistance
translatedContent: - **AI-Assisted Development** — This project was built with AI assistance
